Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 11,983,197.55 moved across the accounts in this case.
  • RADHA REKHA DUBEY sent out Rs 7,740,425.81.
  • Money was routed through 1 intermediary account.
  • DEEPA CHAUHAN spread money to 289 different accounts.
  • DEEPA CHAUHAN collected money from 111 different accounts.
  • Rs 6,925,187.16 of incoming funds was traced to onward transfers or withdrawals.
  • The account to investigate first is YASH DUBEY.

YASH DUBEY strong
Account Number 25078124219247 · DCB Bank · Score 5,437
Rank #1

The account YASH DUBEY (25078124219247) exhibits suspicious money behavior, characterized by a large credit of 400000.00 and subsequent tracing into 17 debits, as well as multiple structuring smurfing transactions totaling 570545.00. This pattern of transactions raises concerns about potential money laundering activities. The account's transactions demonstrate a high level of complexity and potential evasion tactics.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 400000.00 was traced into 17 subsequent debits with no remaining untraced amount, indicating a potentially suspicious money trail. (+52 more of this type)
  • Structuring Smurfing Detection strong — 79 credit transactions totaling 570545.00 were made on 2025-03-15, with each transaction below the individual cutoff, suggesting structuring smurfing activity.
  • Duplicate Verification weak — Transactions ref:681310110010629 share an account, direction, near-equal amount, and nearby date, but were not flagged as duplicates, potentially indicating missed duplicate transactions. (+2 more of this type)
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph with 837 nodes and 2233 directed transaction edges was constructed, indicating a complex web of transactions that may be worthy of further investigation.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — 9 suspicious round-value debits totaling 277000.00 were made, with repeated amounts of 25,000, potentially indicating a pattern of high-value transactions designed to evade detection.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
